title: FIR Registration under BNSS Section 173
description: Registration of First Information Report (FIR). Online/electronic FIRs must be signed by the informant within 3 days.
remediation_guidance: Ensure the electronic FIR is signed by the informant/complainant within 3 days of submission to convert it into a formal FIR.

title: Preliminary Inquiry under BNSS Section 173(3)
description: Preliminary inquiry for cognizable offenses punishable with 3 to 7 years must be completed within 14 days to decide if a prima facie case exists.
remediation_guidance: Complete the preliminary inquiry and record findings within 14 days of receipt of information before registering the FIR or closing the inquiry.

title: Search and Seizure Authorization (BNSS Section 185)
description: Search of place and seizure of items must be authorized by warrant, or recorded grounds of belief if search is conducted without warrant.
remediation_guidance: Obtain search warrant from Magistrate, or document and register 'Reasons to Believe' in the Case Diary before conducting warrantless search.

title: Seizure Memo and Witness Signatures (BNSS Section 185)
description: A detailed Seizure Memo must be prepared at the scene, listing all items seized, and signed by at least two independent respectable inhabitants of the locality.
remediation_guidance: Prepare a seizure list at the search location, obtain signatures of at least two independent witnesses, and deliver a copy to the occupant.

title: Witness Statements under BNSS Section 180
description: Examination of witnesses by police during investigation, with statements recorded in writing.
remediation_guidance: Record detailed statements of witnesses, reduce them to writing (digitally or physically), and log them in the Case Diary. Do not obtain witness signatures on these police statements.

title: Magistrate Statement Recording (BNSS Section 183)
description: Statements or confessions of victims/witnesses recorded by a Magistrate, especially required for specified offenses.
remediation_guidance: Apply to the Magistrate for recording statements of key witnesses or victims under Section 183 BNSS, particularly in cases involving sexual offenses or major frauds.

title: Medical Examination of Accused (BNSS Section 184)
description: Medical examination of the arrested person must be conducted by a registered medical practitioner within 24 hours of arrest.
remediation_guidance: Conduct medical examination of the arrested person immediately after arrest and obtain a copy of the medical report signed by the practitioner.

title: Arrest Memo and Relatives Notification (BNSS Section 48)
description: Arrest memo must be prepared, signed by at least one witness (member of family or respectable locality inhabitant), counter-signed by the arrested person, and relatives notified.
remediation_guidance: Prepare Arrest Memo, obtain witness and arrestee signatures, log date/time, and formally notify the designated family member or friend of the arrest.

title: Magistrate Presentation within 24 Hours (BNSS Section 58)
description: Every arrested person must be produced before the nearest Magistrate within 24 hours of arrest, excluding travel time.
remediation_guidance: Ensure the arrested person is presented in person before the Magistrate within 24 hours of arrest. If travel delay occurs, document travel times in the transit memo.

title: Chargesheet Filing Timeline (BNSS Section 193)
description: Filing of final report/chargesheet before the Magistrate. Typically within 60 days (for offenses punishable with under 10 years) or 90 days (for death/life/over 10 years).
remediation_guidance: Prepare and submit the final chargesheet report under Section 193 BNSS within 60 or 90 days from the date of arrest of the accused to prevent default bail.

title: BSA 2023 Section 63 Certificate for Digital Evidence
description: Digital/electronic records require an admissibility certificate under Section 63 of the Bharatiya Sakshya Adhiniyam 2023.
remediation_guidance: Obtain BSA Section 63 certificate signed by the person in charge of the computer resource or device, verifying the cryptographic hash and operating condition of the system.

title: Disclosure of Evidence to Accused (BNSS Section 230)
description: Provide copies of the police report, FIR, statements of witnesses, and all other relevant documents/electronic records to the accused.
remediation_guidance: Furnish the accused with complete copies of all documents and digital evidence (on USB/CD or secure access link) within the prescribed time under Section 230/258 BNSS.

title: Forensic Laboratory Request Submission
description: All seized items requiring forensic examination must be submitted to the forensic science laboratory with a formal request letter, seizure memo, and chain of custody documentation.
remediation_guidance: Prepare formal laboratory request with FSL reference number, attach seizure memo, and document chain of custody from scene to lab. Retain acknowledgment receipt from the laboratory.

title: Expert Report Receipt and Documentation
description: Expert opinions on seized material (digital forensics, handwriting, DNA, chemical analysis) must be obtained and documented in the case file before chargesheet submission.
remediation_guidance: Follow up with the forensic laboratory for pending reports. Ensure expert credentials and appointment documents are attached to the case file.

title: Digital Device Acquisition and Imaging Log
description: All digital devices must be forensically imaged using write-blockers. The acquisition log must record: device description, serial number, imaging tool used, hash values (MD5/SHA-256), operator name, date/time, and witness details.
remediation_guidance: Image all digital devices using forensic tools (FTK Imager, dd, Cellebrite). Record hash values before and after imaging. Have two independent witnesses sign the acquisition log.

title: Scene of Crime Documentation
description: Scene of Crime (SOC) report must include photographs, video recording, sketch/map of the scene, and detailed narrative of observations. For cyber crimes, this includes screenshots of online content and server locations.
remediation_guidance: Prepare SOC report with timestamped photographs, video walkthrough, and detailed narrative. For cyber scenes, capture screenshots with URL bars visible, page source, and server IP addresses.

title: Notice to Suspect under BNSS Section 35
description: Notice to any person against whom a reasonable complaint has been made, requiring their appearance for examination.
remediation_guidance: Issue formal notice under BNSS Section 35 specifying date, time, and place of appearance. Serve notice through proper channel and retain proof of service.

title: Interim Victim Compensation Application (BNSS Section 396)
description: Application for interim compensation to the victim may be filed during the investigation stage itself.
remediation_guidance: File application for interim victim compensation before the Magistrate with supporting documentation of financial loss or injury suffered by the victim.
